heading: Discharge of officers with less than six years of commissioned service or found not qualified for promotion to first lieutenant or lieutenant (junior grade)
label: 14503 Discharge of officers with less than six years of commissioned service or found not qualified for promotion to first lieutenant or lieutenant (junior grade)
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in subsecs. (a) and (b) of this section were contained in sections 3819(b) and 8819(c) of this title, prior to repeal by , (c)(2).Pub. L. 103–337, § 1629(a)(2)

L. 103–337section 10001 of this titlesection 14503 of title 10

children.text: has less than six years of service in an active status as a commissioned officer; or
is serving in the grade of second lieutenant or ensign and has been found not qualified for promotion to the grade of first lieutenant or lieutenant (junior grade).

children.label: A
children.text: may be discharged at any time after being found not qualified for promotion; and

children.label: B
children.text: if not sooner discharged, shall be discharged at the end of the 18-month period beginning on the date on which the officer is first found not qualified for promotion.
Paragraph (1) shall not apply if the officer is sooner promoted.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: c .—Regulations
children.text: Discharges under this section shall be made under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense and may be made without regard to .section 12645 of this title
